category_title: Male adolescents at risk of dropping out in vocational training programs
student_description: This student is a male vocational learner (electrician) with a history of academic failure and low self-esteem who quickly disengages from pure theory or rigid, prescriptive tasks. The evidence reveals he uniquely needs practical, real-world connections to his daily life or profession, autonomy to make decisions, and a safe, unhurried environment to explore difficulties and self-assess.

criteria.rationale: Over time, these students need to see the ongoing utility of theoretical concepts in their daily lives to maintain engagement and overcome their history of academic failure.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan consistently anchors future modules and theoretical concepts in practical, real-world scenarios or daily life issues.

criteria.rationale: Joint assessment helps these students safely evaluate their own difficulties and build self-esteem through constructive, comparative feedback.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan incorporates a structured routine where the student evaluates their own competencies and compares them with the tutor's feedback.
